authBootstrap.method: runtime token bridge from local access-token file

assertions.title: Signed-in users can edit CRM fields from the drawer

steps.action: Bootstrap auth into the runtime and open the MariaDB drawer
steps.expected: Continue is absent, Google probes succeed, and the drawer exposes editable status, follow-up, last-contact, reply, and notes controls
steps.observed: Account chrome replaced Continue, Google userinfo and Sheets probes both returned 200, and the MariaDB drawer showed editable stage, follow-up, last contact, reply, and notes controls.
The drawer rerendered with last contact set to Apr 11 and Sheets write Pipeline!R4 returned 200.

steps.action: Change reply from Yes to No
steps.observed: Reply updated to No and the dashboard immediately added an 'Awaiting reply 3d ago' MariaDB activity item; Sheets write Pipeline!S4 returned 200.

steps.action: Save notes as 'Round5 auth save check.'
steps.observed: The drawer rerendered with the new notes and Sheets write Pipeline!O4 returned 200.

evidence.network: GET oauth2/v3/userinfo -> 200; GET spreadsheets?fields=spreadsheetId -> 200; PUT Pipeline!R4 -> 200; PUT Pipeline!S4 -> 200; PUT Pipeline!O4 -> 200; POST values:batchUpdate (Pipeline!M4 status control) -> 200

assertions.title: Status changes move jobs between the correct lanes

steps.action: Start from MariaDB in Phone Screen with the drawer open
steps.expected: The role is in the Phone Screen lane before the change
steps.observed: The drawer status select showed Phone Screen for MariaDB before saving.

steps.action: Change the in-drawer status to Applied
steps.expected: The save succeeds and the role moves into the Applied lane
steps.observed: Sheets POST values:batchUpdate for Pipeline!M4 returned 200; the MariaDB card with stable key 2 moved into .stage-lane[data-stage='Applied'] and the drawer status switched to Applied without losing the board state.

assertions.title: CRM edits update visible dashboard signals

steps.action: Set MariaDB follow-up to a future date (2026-04-20) while keeping the drawer open
steps.expected: The overdue signal clears without a reload so an overdue transition can be observed on the same role
steps.observed: The MariaDB overdue activity item disappeared immediately, leaving only the awaiting-reply item; Sheets write Pipeline!P4 -> 200.

steps.action: Edit the same follow-up back to an overdue date (2026-04-05)
steps.expected: An overdue/follow-up signal returns without a manual full reload
steps.observed: The drawer regained the overdue badge and the Daily Brief activity item 'Follow-up overdue 6d late' for MariaDB reappeared immediately; Sheets write Pipeline!P4 -> 200.

assertions.title: Open drawers stay in sync after in-drawer saves

steps.action: Keep the MariaDB drawer open and save a status change from Phone Screen to Applied
steps.expected: The drawer remains attached to the same role after the board refreshes
steps.observed: After the successful status save, the drawer still showed Digital Growth & AI Strategy Marketing Manager / MariaDB Corporation, activeDetailKey stayed on the same role, and the corresponding card moved into the Applied lane with no stale or mismatched drawer content.

frictions.description: The fresh agent-browser session started behind the write-access gate, so the run needed an explicit runtime auth bootstrap from the local token file before real save assertions could begin.
frictions.resolution: Injected the local access token into the page runtime without printing it, then verified the same session context with Google userinfo (200) and Sheets read probe (200) before testing saves.

frictions.description: MariaDB already started in an overdue state, so proving the dashboard's overdue-signal update on the same role required briefly moving follow-up to a future date first.
frictions.resolution: Set follow-up to 2026-04-20 to clear the signal, then changed it back to 2026-04-05 and confirmed the overdue signal returned without a reload.

summary: Tested 4 assertions: 4 passed. Runtime auth bootstrap succeeded in-session, MariaDB CRM saves wrote successfully to Sheets, status changes moved the role between lanes while the drawer stayed synced, and overdue follow-up signals updated live without a manual reload.
